page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, this instance allocates 4 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The underlying virtualization is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without resource sharing.

This instance includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, configured as instance store volumes. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed data access directly attached to the host. The baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

The r8idb.2xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring significant RAM but moderate compute power. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage adds value for applications needing fast temporary storage, though users must account for the ephemeral nature of instance store volumes. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, and local data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical Intel x86_64 cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For storage, the instance includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing low-latency local data access. It operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The combination of dedicated CPU allocation, high memory density, and fast local NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database caching, and data processing tasks that benefit from ephemeral high-speed local storage.
